title: Kraftigt fall på börserna – oljepriset lyfter
summary_md:
# Kraftigt fall på börserna – oljepriset lyfter

**Feed**: efn-borsfrukost  
**Published**: 2026-06-08  
**Market Sentiment**: Negative

⏱️ **Time Horizon Key**: ST = <3 months | MT = 3-12 months | LT = >12 months

---

## Episode Thesis

Episode covers a sharp, broad sell-off across global markets driven by an 'AI-frossa' (AI fever/correction) hitting semiconductors, a broken Middle East ceasefire lifting Brent crude above $97, and a now priced-in Fed rate hike following strong US jobs data. Crypto is weak with Bitcoin in a bear market near the key $60k support, pressured by ETF outflows and capital rotating into AI IPOs (SpaceX, OpenAI, Anthropic), though regulatory tailwinds (Genius Act, Clarity Act) remain constructive. Defensive telecoms outperform while Saab is supported by a rumoured large Canadian Gripen order.

---

## Universe Stocks

### 🟢 SAAB-B — Saab | **BUY** (Moderate)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: MT  
**Confidence**: 55%

Rumoured large Canadian order for 72 Gripen E jets (>2x Colombia order); falling less than the market and hosts find the sell-off counterintuitive given worsening geopolitics.

#### Reasoning

Hosts argue the sell-off in Saab is strange: a potential Canadian Gripen order would be a major catalyst (more than double the prior Colombia deal), and worsening Middle East conflict has historically benefited defense stocks. Defense had cooled after last year's hot run, but hosts suggest Saab and sector peers could come back into focus.

#### Sell Triggers

- if the Canadian Gripen order fails to materialise
- if Middle East tensions de-escalate sharply
- if broad market risk-off deepens into a sustained sell-off

> *"Att Saab ska handlas ner känns ändå lite underligt. Inte minst med tanke på att det pratas så mycket om geopolitisk oro och krigsutveckling som det är värre. Tidigare har det gynnat försvarsaktier."*

---

### ⛔ SIVE — Sivers Semiconductor | **AVOID** (Moderate)

**Sentiment**: Negative | **Time Horizon**: ST  
**Confidence**: 50%

Hyped stock with weak institutional conviction; JP Morgan >5% stake of unclear nature; negative narrative reinforced by critics and short-sellers questioning valuation.

#### Reasoning

Hosts frame Sivers as a hyped name not widely believed by institutions, down ~3% (more than the market). A JP Morgan >5% holding is ambiguous (possibly aggregated US retail rather than a strategic bank position). A rough week of negative news flow, critics and short-sellers has reinforced concerns about the company's valuation, though it ticked back to flat intraday.

#### Sell Triggers

- if JP Morgan stake confirmed as a strategic institutional position
- if positive operational/order news emerges
- if market sentiment turns risk-on

> *"Det är ett hajpat bolag. Inte något som institutioner tror på i särskilt stor utsträckning."*

---

### 🔴 HM-B — H&M | **SELL** (Moderate)

**Sentiment**: Negative | **Time Horizon**: MT  
**Confidence**: 60%

Nordea resumed coverage with a sell recommendation; stock down ~2%.

#### Reasoning

Cited analyst signal: Nordea resumed coverage of H&M with, among other things, a sell recommendation, and the stock fell ~2% on the day amid the broad market weakness.

#### Sell Triggers

- if Nordea upgrades the rating
- if upcoming sales/results beat expectations

> *"Där så har vi sett att Nordea återupp tar bevakning av aktien med en säljrekommendation bland annat."*

---

### ⛔ ZEAL — Zealand Pharma | **AVOID** (Weak)

**Sentiment**: Mixed | **Time Horizon**: MT  
**Confidence**: 45%

Stock crashed >20% on obesity study results; strong efficacy (16.6% weight loss, large fat reductions) overshadowed by ~1 in 5 patients dropping out due to side effects.

#### Reasoning

Despite Zealand presenting what hosts read as positive data from its key obesity study (16.6% weight loss; organ fat -34%, liver fat -61% at 76 weeks), the market reacted negatively, with the stock down over 20%, because nearly one in five patients had to discontinue due to side effects (nausea, vomiting, diarrhea). Tolerability concerns dominate the read-through.

#### Sell Triggers

- if later data show improved tolerability
- if regulatory/partnership news reassures on the side-effect profile

> *"Markant ras Zeland Pharma presenterade på en konferens i fredags... nästan var femte person fick avbryta den här studien på grund av biverkningar."*

---

### 🟡 TELIA — Telia | **HOLD** (Weak)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: ST  
**Confidence**: 40%

Defensive name rising against a sharply falling market.

#### Reasoning

Hosts highlight Telia as a classic defensive that is bucking the trend, up ~0.5-0.8% on a very dark day for the market. Framed as a defensive holding rather than an active conviction call.

#### Sell Triggers

- if market risk appetite recovers and rotation back to risk assets occurs

> *"Jag vill nämna att det som går mot strömmen just nu är defensiva Telia och Tele2. Telia är upp 0,8 och Tele2 är upp 0,2."*

---

### 🟡 TEL2-B — Tele2 | **HOLD** (Weak)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: ST  
**Confidence**: 40%

Defensive telecom rising against the broad market decline.

#### Reasoning

Tele2 cited alongside Telia as a defensive that goes against the stream during the sell-off, up modestly. A defensive hold call rather than strong conviction.

#### Sell Triggers

- if market sentiment turns risk-on and capital rotates out of defensives

> *"Motströmmen fortsätter till 2 och Telia som är upp runt 0,5 procent vardera."*

---

### 🟠 ERIC-B — Ericsson | **WATCH** (Weak)

**Sentiment**: Negative | **Time Horizon**: ST  
**Confidence**: 45%

Down ~3% and sold off in the US late last week alongside Nokia; hosts flag it as worth watching closely amid tech weakness.

#### Reasoning

Both Ericsson and Nokia were sold off sharply in the US at the end of last week, prompting hosts to say it is worth keeping extra watch on the tech names amid the broad semiconductor/tech 'AI-frossa' correction.

#### Sell Triggers

- if the tech/AI sell-off intensifies
- if Q results disappoint

> *"Både Ericsson och Nokia handlades ner rätt saftigt i USA i slutet av förra veckan så det är värt att hålla extra koll på det."*

---
